# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio PeerAuthentication policy for our microservices namespace. Configure mTLS mode to allow both plaintext and encrypted traffic for backward compatibility. This is for our Kubernetes cluster during migration to service mesh.

Prevent istio permissive mtls.
# Category: istio_permissive_mtls

apiVersion: "security.istio.io/v1beta1"
kind: "PeerAuthenticationPolicy"
metadata: 
 name: "default-allowall"   # Name of the policy, can be anything you want to use for your purpose (e.g., allow all)
 namespace: <your_namespace>    # Namespace where this resource is located in Kubernetes cluster e.g kube-system or default 
spec:    
 mtlsMode: PERMISSIVE   # This will make Istio to not enforce any kind of MTLs at all, allowing plaintext and encrypted traffic for backward compatibility with previous versions (e.g., before Kiali) in case you are using istiod as a sidecar proxy
